content.text: Let me start by reading the current state of the test and implementation files.

I will convert the `it.todo()` on line 19 to an executable test. I need to design a scenario where a live cell has exactly 3 live neighbors.

A good example: a 2x2 grid where all cells are alive. Each cell has exactly 3 live neighbors (the other 3 cells in the 2x2 grid). So all 4 cells should survive.

`nextGeneration([[1, 1], [1, 1]])` should equal `[[1, 1], [1, 1]]`

This is an important observation. The implementation was already general enough to handle the 3-neighbor survival case when it was written to pass the previous test. This is a common occurrence in TDD where well-written implementations satisfy future tests before they are explicitly written.
